name: Shrimp on Sugar Cane (Chao Tom)
recipeIngredient: 1 t Rock sugar, pounded to a powder, or 1 teaspoon granulated sugar
2 Egg whites, beaten until slightly frothy
2 tbsp Pork fat, boiled for 10 minutes and diced very small
1 t Rock sugar, pounded to a powder, or 1 teaspoon granulated sugar
2 Egg whites, beaten until slightly frothy
2 tbsp Pork fat, boiled for 10 minutes and diced very small
recipeInstructions:
The "tuong" referred to below is a Vietnamese soybean preparation—a kind of thin, salty paste. If you can't find it, you might get away with substituting Chinese bean sauce, mashed and thinned a bit with water, or possibly a dark Japanese miso. Although you can make this in a food processor or blender, it's best to pound it in a mortar with a pestle to achieve that certain crunchiness which is a most desirable quality of much Vietnamese food.
1. Mash the garlic in a mortar until smooth. Add the shrimp one at a time and mash to a paste. If necessary, remove some of the prepared shrimp paste to make room for additional shrimp.
2. Pound the remaining shrimp into a smooth paste with the pestle.
3. Reduce the sugar into the shrimp paste and pound it together.
4. Add the egg whites and continue pounding until well blended.
5. Incorporate the roasted rice powder, black pepper, and pork fat, combining all ingredients thoroughly.
6. Peel the sugar cane, cut into 4-inch lengths, then split lengthwise into quarters.
7. Pour about 1/4 cup of oil into a bowl. Dip your fingers in the oil and mold it into an oval shape around the sugar cane, leaving half exposed for serving as a handle.
8. Place all molded shapes on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 30 minutes or until brown.
9. Serve with vegetable platter, dried rice papers, and nuoc leo with tamarind:
- Each person is given a dried rice paper. Dip the entire surface in water to make it soft and flexible.
- Add lettuce, cucumber, coriander, and mint from the vegetable platter.
- Place shrimp patty on top of vegetables, forming a cylinder at one end.
- Fold each side over to enclose the filling and roll up.
- Dip in small bowl of sauce. Enjoy with sugar cane.
This dish makes 6 servings.
NUOC LEO WITH TAMARIND:
- Soak tamarind paste in 3 tablespoons water. Heat oil, add chopped garlic until fragrant.
- Add soaked tamarind liquid to the sauce, discard remaining paste.
- Stir in tuong, sugar, peanut butter; cook for 2 minutes.
- Sprinkle nuts on top and serve.
ROASTED RICE (THINH):
- Toast rice in a dry pan over high heat until brown. Transfer to blender and grind into powder. Store as directed.
